Introduction.name: Juyeon Won
Introduction.body: I single-handedly own the development and maintenance of the security product PrivKeeper P, implementing device integrations, concurrency handling, and security features hands-on. Lately I've been extending into RAG/LLM-based automation.

About.title: About Me
About.paragraph-1: I'm a backend & security engineer who, when I hit friction or repetitive problems at work, doesn't just let them slide — I build tools to make things more convenient and productive.
About.paragraph-2: For example, instead of analyzing after the fact why each camera's password change failed, I built OnviProbe, a tool that predicts the outcome in advance; and to quickly inspect broadcast-encryption streams in the field, I built utilApp, now in real internal use.
About.paragraph-3: I applied the same mindset to repetitive customer-incident handling, turning it into a tool that auto-classifies inquiries and drafts responses with an LLM. I pick up new tech by asking, 'what can I make easier with this?'
About.paragraph-4: As my main work, I solely own PrivKeeper P, a password-management security solution — security-function certification, maintenance, field support, and new development — and I'm currently owning the RAG/LLM part of a government R&D VLM project.
About.paragraph-5: When something breaks, I narrow the root cause down to the end using logs and raw payloads — and along the way I'm always thinking, 'next time, I could automate this.'

privkeeperp.title: PrivKeeper P — Password-Management Security Solution (main work)
privkeeperp.description: A security product that automatically manages accounts and passwords across diverse devices (cameras, VMS, network gear, servers). I own it end to end — development, security certification, and on-site support — and my strength is digging into device/protocol problems from evidence while keeping it secure and stable.

onviprobe.title: OnviProbe — ONVIF Camera Diagnostic Tool
onviprobe.description: A Go tool I built on my own initiative to cut down on recurring field issues — it predicts whether PrivKeeper can change a given camera's password without touching the camera. It reproduces the product's ONVIF decision logic, adds an independent ONVIF-standard verdict, and categorizes failure causes into codes.

utilapp.title: utilApp — Broadcast Encryption (CAS) TS/EMM Analyzer
utilapp.description: A tool I built that's now in real internal use — a Qt/C++ diagnostic app that lets you check, on site, whether a broadcast (CAS) stream is encrypted and whether EMM is being received. Another case of 'if it's inconvenient, I build it.'

aiauto.title: PrivKeeper Incident-Response Automation (AI · LLM)
aiauto.description: An internal automation tool I built while learning to apply LLMs — it auto-classifies customer inquiries/incidents with Gemini·GPT and drafts responses and similar-case references.

cctv.title: CCTV Anomaly-Response RAG Solution (Government R&D · VLM Project)
cctv.description: Owning the RAG/LLM part of a government R&D VLM project. Building a RAG knowledge base from guideline documents and designing an auto-generation pipeline (ChromaDB + embeddings + local LLM) for situational action recommendations and reports; currently preparing for performance evaluation.

more.text: Most of the projects above are internal/security products, so the source is private.

Contact.title: Contact Me
Contact.intro: If you'd like to collaborate on a product, or reach out about a role,
Contact.cta: you can contact me at wjy6454@naver.com or via the links below.

bullets: After joining, contributed to GS (Good Software) certification and project wrap-up of PrivKeeper M — a C# product that masks license plates and faces in video and re-saves it as a de-identified file.
Then took sole ownership of PrivKeeper P, a password-management security solution — security-function certification, maintenance, field support, and new feature development.
Work broadly across heterogeneous device integration (cameras, VMS, network gear) and security features (encryption, authentication, sessions), keeping the product stable.
Currently owning the RAG/LLM part of a government R&D VLM project.
